summary: Jeff Atwood shares a Visual Studio .NET macro he created that allows developers to search Google for any highlighted text directly within the IDE. Unlike similar macros that only work in code windows, this version works with any highlighted text including output from the Output window. The post provides the complete VB.NET macro code along with step-by-step installation instructions for both VS.NET 2003 and 2005. Atwood recommends binding the macro to ALT+F1 as a natural extension of the F1 help key.
key_points: The macro improves on existing solutions by working with highlighted text from any IDE window, not just code editors
The implementation handles multiple window types including the Output window and HTML editor views
ALT+F1 is suggested as an intuitive keyboard shortcut since it extends the F1 help key pattern
The macro is cross-compatible between VS.NET 2003 and 2005 without modifications
money_quotes: I know what you're thinking: you've seen this macro before. Yeah, but this one goes to eleven.
It actually works with any highlighted text in the IDE – including highlighted text from the Output window
It's really quite handy; ALT+F1 is a totally natural chord and a logical superset of F1
key_insight: Small developer tool improvements that work consistently across all contexts—like searching from any IDE window rather than just code—provide outsized productivity gains.
